title: shorea zeylanica
intro: Shorea zeylanica, also known as East Indian rosewood or Rajanaka, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to the tropical forests of Asia. The tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities, which have been attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Shorea zeylanica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource for the treatment of various health conditions. In traditional medicine, Shorea zeylanica is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used as an essential oil, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
